title: Railroad crossing warning system
abstract: A railroad crossing warning system includes a beam emitter for generating a focused beam of energy, and a receiver for receiving the focused beam of energy. A pair of weatherproof housings secures the emitter and receiver diagonally at a railroad intersection. A base station is communicatively linked to the beam emitter and the receiver, and a base station communication unit transmits an obstruction warning to an approaching train.
claims: Claims Claims Claim 1. A railroad crossing warning system for use at railroad crossings having a pair of crossing gates, said system comprising: a beam emitter that is configured to generate a focused beam of energy; a receiver that is configured to receive the focused beam of energy; and a base station that is in communication with each of the beam emitter and the receiver, said base station including functionality for determining that the focused beam of energy is not being received by the receiver, and for broadcasting an obstruction warning upon the determination. Claim 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the focused beam of energy includes a laser signal. Claim 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the focused beam of energy includes an infrared signal. Claim 4. The system of claim 1, further comprising: a first waterproof housing having an interior space for receiving the beam emitter; a second waterproof housing having an interior space for receiving the receiver; each of the first and second waterproof housings including a clear surface for allowing the focused beam of energy to pass therethrough. Claim 5. The system of claim 4, wherein each of the first and second waterproof housings include a crossing gate connector for mating with the pair of crossing gates at a railroad intersection. Claim 6. The system of claim 4, wherein each of the beam emitter and the receiver are configured to be electrically connected to one of the pair of crossing gates, and to receive electrical power when an arm of the respective crossing gate is in the down position. Claim 7. The system of claim 1, wherein the base station comprises: a memory for storing instructions; a component interface for communicating with the beam emitter and receiver; and a communication unit for communicating wirelessly with an external object. Claim 8. The system of claim 7, wherein the communication unit includes a variable radio transmitter that is configured to broadcast an audible alert at a first frequency and for a first distance. Claim 9. The system of claim 8, wherein the first distance is between approximately 0.1 and 0.5 miles. Claim 10. A railroad crossing warning system for use at railroad crossings, said system comprising: a first freestanding weatherproof housing having a clear surface along one end; a second freestanding weatherproof housing having a clear surface along one end; a beam emitter that is configured to generate a focused beam of energy, said beam emitter being located within the first freestanding housing; a receiver that is configured to receive the focused beam of energy, said receiver being located within the second freestanding housing; and a base station that is in communication with each of the beam emitter and the receiver, said base station including functionality for determining that the focused beam of energy is not being received by the receiver, and for broadcasting an obstruction warning upon the determination. Claim 11. The system of claim 10, further comprising: a solar power unit that is configured to supply a power requirement to each of the base station, the beam emitter and the receiver. Claim 12. The system of claim 10, wherein the focused beam of energy includes a laser signal. Claim 13. The system of claim 10, wherein the focused beam of energy includes an infrared signal. Claim 14. The system of claim 10, wherein the base station comprises: a memory for storing instructions; a component interface for communicating with the beam emitter and receiver; and a communication unit for communicating wirelessly with an external object. Claim 15. The system of claim 14, wherein the communication unit includes a variable radio transmitter that is configured to broadcast an audible alert at a first frequency and for a first distance. Claim 16. The system of claim 15, wherein the first distance is between approximately 0.1 and 0.5 miles.
